market: futures_index (ES-class) / SPY-class liquid ETF / liquid FX majors (ablation-test scope matches the instruments that rejected intraday_momo)
timeframe: 5m/15m intraday bars, one decision per RTH session
data_source: TradingView OHLCV via the DT Intraday Momentum Pure v1 Pine indicator/strategy pair, cross-checked against the source academic papers (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou JFE 1993-2013; Baltussen et al. 1974-2020 global futures extension) for the unadapted signal logic; the exit model (no stop, hold to session close) is a direct replication of the source studies' tested behavior, not an untested overlay. The optional disaster stop, when enabled, is untested by any source and defaults off.
entry: At the decision bar T (default 30 minutes before session close, e.g. 15:30 ET for a 16:00 ET close), compute daySoFarReturn = close(T) / session_open_price - 1.
If open30Return and daySoFarReturn are both non-zero and share the same sign, enter long when the sign is positive and short when the sign is negative, at the close of the decision bar T.
No entry if either return is exactly zero or the signs disagree.
Exactly one entry per session per instrument; no re-entry after the decision bar even if conditions would otherwise qualify.
Identical entry logic to intraday_momo -- this spec exists to run a single-variable ablation test on the exit model only, per intraday_momo_spec.json's diagnostic_test requirement.
exit_take_profit: None. No partial-exit order and no take-profit limit order exist in this variant. The full position is held to a hard time exit at session close, replicating the source studies' tested 'let it run to close' behavior exactly (this is the entire hypothesis being tested: that intraday_momo's TP1 50%-off overlay was cutting this tail).
The indicator label's tp1=/tp2= fields are numeric informational-only ATR-multiple projections (1.0x / 2.0x ATR(14) from entry) for chart reference; they are never real order prices.
exit_stop_loss: None by default. Matches the source studies exactly (no native stop-loss in either source paper).

Optional catastrophic-only 'disaster stop' input (ATR multiple, default 0 = off). When enabled (>0), a single full-position stop order is placed at that ATR distance from entry -- this is a tail-risk backstop only, not a replication of intraday_momo's removed 1.5x ATR SL, and must be sized wide enough that it is not expected to fire under the tested run-to-close behavior. Any local backtest run with the disaster stop enabled must be recorded as a separate variant from the pure (disaster-stop-off) run in journal/backtests/, per the negative_result_log requirement below.
position_size: Configurable at runtime: 'Fixed quantity' (default, 1 contract/share) or 'Percent of equity' (10% default), selected via a strategy input and passed through qty= on every strategy.entry() call. Default changed from intraday_momo's static percent_of_equity because intraday_momo's futures backtests hit a silent zero-trade sizing trap (10% of $1,000,000 is smaller than one ES/NQ/YM contract's notional) that required a manual fixed_1_contract override to produce real trade counts.

risk.max_risk_per_trade: Unbounded per trade unless the disaster stop is enabled -- this is an intentional deviation from intraday_momo's bounded 1.5x ATR risk-per-trade and is the direct consequence of testing the source studies' actual no-stop design. If the disaster stop is enabled for a given backtest/paper run, risk per trade is capped at the disaster-stop ATR distance times position size; this must be recorded explicitly per run since it changes the tested behavior.
risk.daily_loss_limit: 1.0% equity as a portfolio-level circuit breaker across concurrently-run instrument instances (moot for a single instrument at 1 signal/day, same rationale as intraday_momo_spec.json)
kill_switch: Live/paper P&L diverges materially from the pure (no-overlay) backtest for 10 consecutive sessions

A single session's unrealized drawdown on an open pure (disaster-stop-off) position exceeds 5x the intraday_momo overlay's SL distance (1.5x ATR) -- flags that the no-stop design is producing outsized single-trade losses beyond what the ablation test intended to observe
TradingView CDP/data feed failure preventing decision-bar or EOD-exit detection
Manual override required before any live-sizing promotion beyond paper trading

benchmark: intraday_momo (the overlaid sibling setup) over the identical instruments/timeframes/dates -- this is the primary comparison, since the entire purpose of this spec is a single-variable ablation test
Buy-and-hold ES continuous futures (or SPY) over the same dates

validation.negative_result_log: Preserve the parameters and metrics for each failed, negative, rejected, or underperforming variant tested locally (e.g. disaster stop on vs. off, alternate disaster-stop ATR multiples, alternate decision-time offsets, fixed-quantity vs. percent-of-equity sizing, SPY-ETF vs. index-futures instrument choice) in journal/backtests/ per variant/instrument/timeframe run, not only in aggregate. Every run must record whether the disaster stop was enabled and at what ATR multiple.
validation.diagnostic_test: This spec records the counter-thesis for intraday_momo's failure cause: the SL/TP1 overlay is cutting the run-to-close tail that produces the edge. To test it, run one single-variable ablation test: remove the SL/TP1 overlay (this setup, disaster stop off) and compare only-EOD-exit performance against the prior intraday_momo backtest evidence on the same instruments and timeframes (ES/NQ/YM futures and EURUSD/GBPUSD/USDJPY/AUDUSD FX at 15m minimum). Record the observed, actual difference in PF/WR/trade-count/max-DD as the primary comparison metric. If PF/WR do not materially improve, the counter-thesis is falsified and the underlying signal, not the overlay, should be treated as the likely cause of the earlier negative verdict.
validation.point_in_time_data: Single-instrument system: n/a for universe membership. OHLCV bars are available at the decision time T, matching process_orders_on_close=true.
validation.parameter_freeze: Freeze the disaster-stop ATR multiple (or confirm it stays disabled), the opening-window length, and the decision-time offset before opening the 2025-01-01..2026-06-30 holdout window; no holdout-driven retuning of any parameter after the freeze.

execution.primary_fill_model: Fill at the close of the decision bar T (process_orders_on_close=true, matching the DT ORB v1 / intraday_momo reference convention); EOD exit fills at the close of the last in-session bar.
execution.stress_fill_model: Conservative one-bar-delayed fill at the following bar open, to approximate slippage beyond the decision-bar close.
execution.commission: 0.005% per side primary (matching the intraday_momo reference strategy header) and 0.02% per side stress.
execution.spread: 1 tick primary (ES-class index futures) or 1 cent (SPY-class ETF) or the pair's typical spread (FX) and 3 ticks / 3 cents / 3x spread adverse stress.
execution.slippage: 2 ticks primary (matching the strategy header's slippage=2) and 5 ticks adverse stress.
execution.leverage_funding: 1x gross leverage for the ETF variant; standard index-futures margin (no additional leverage beyond exchange initial margin) for the futures variant; standard retail FX margin for the FX variant. Funding 0 bps for the cash-ETF variant; futures/FX funding (roll/basis, swap) is not separately modeled.

robustness.top_trade_removal: Recompute annualized return and Sharpe after removing the single largest winning trade, and again after removing the top 5% of winning trades, per instrument. Given this variant removes the TP1 partial exit, expect higher single-trade PnL concentration than intraday_momo -- report the comparison explicitly.
regime_splits: trend/range (ADX-based classification of the RTH session)
high/low volatility (VIX or realized-vol tercile split)
COVID-crash subperiod vs. non-crash subperiod (the source paper's OOS retest found the edge concentrated in the COVID period, Sharpe 1.452 vs. -0.628 overall)
robustness.long_short_decomposition: Report long and short trade counts, win rates, and average R separately for both the futures and ETF/FX variants; this setup is not long-only so both sides must be reported.
robustness.concentration_check: Report the top 1, top 3, and top 5 winning-day PnL contribution as a percentage of total strategy PnL, with a concentration cap of 40% for the top 5 winning days fixed before the holdout period. With the TP1 partial exit removed, concentration is expected to be higher than intraday_momo's equivalent figure; this must be disclosed and compared directly, not treated as an anomaly.

review_cadence: weekly paper review; monthly parameter review
edge_death_condition: The core diagnostic test fails: removing the SL/TP1 overlay does not materially improve PF/WR versus the recorded intraday_momo rejection evidence on the same instruments/timeframes
Live/paper P&L diverges from the pure (no-overlay) backtest for 10 consecutive sessions
Realized OOS Sharpe falls below -1.0 for any tested instrument, echoing the documented 2010-2018 SPY negative replication (Sharpe -2.70)
Regime mismatch: edge only present during extreme-volatility periods (e.g. only positive during a COVID-like crash subperiod) rather than persisting across the full holdout
A single-trade loss (unbounded when the disaster stop is off) exceeds the kill_switch threshold above, indicating the no-stop design is not viable for the sizing/instrument in use

human_confirmation.live_orders: manual only; this spec checker never places orders. This setup is a research candidate and must not be marked 'adopted' in journal/registry.json until both the diagnostic ablation test and the holdout period above are completed and independently reviewed.
